search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG202 snoring witnessed apnoea daytime sleepiness assessment
site:entnet.org paediatric sleep-disordered breathing tonsillectomy guideline snoring
candidate_sources_rejected: adult evidence applied to paediatric snoring without qualification
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Snoring alone does not establish a sleep disorder.
The workflow remains partial because age and comorbidity context are not encoded.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with snoring under ENT review; adult and paediatric evidence is kept separate and neither establishes a diagnosis.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, audiology, respiratory, sleep, or ENT assessment as qualified by the reviewed source and workflow purpose.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE ENT, audiology, sleep-service, emergency, prescribing, referral, and local pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections and current source pages were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on its issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The adult exact section supports interval snoring history, witnessed breathing disturbance, sleep quality, daytime impact, functional safety and comorbidity context without asserting OSAHS.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The paediatric exact section supports caregiver-reported snoring and sleep-related comorbidity context plus ENT examination without recommending tonsillectomy or polysomnography.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult follow-up section supports documenting clinician-entered review plans and follow-up without creating device, testing, or restriction advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, caregiver observations, sleep schedule, nasal and tonsillar context, prior tests, established diagnosis, device use, and occupational or driving context remain unsupported unless explicitly documented.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
